content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 74: The human-in-the-loop map

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to workflow and automation: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
STRUCTURING & ORGANISING | PUSH | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Decide where the human must remain in the workflow.

THE SITUATION:
Take a recurring task and map the human and AI roles.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Decide where the human must remain in the workflow. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model to split the workflow into steps: human-only, AI-assisted, AI-draft, human-review, never automate.

2. Ask why each human checkpoint matters.

3. Rewrite the workflow with review points and failure modes.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- Human checkpoints are explicit.
- The workflow includes never-automate zones.
- Review has a purpose beyond rubber-stamping.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to reusable prompts, agent briefs, QA checklists, process specs, human-in-the-loop workflows, and the move from private cleverness to repeatable value.

COACHING NOTE:
Automation without judgement is just outsourcing your mistakes to something faster.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Keep the map for workplace adoption. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
review_notes: Generated as part of the 90-Day AI Habit paid founding track.
Continues from the free 14-day starter sequence without duplicating the foundation.
Organised by professional capability themes: creation, research, decisions, creativity, management, communication, productivity, analysis, workflow, judgement, and workplace adoption.
Draws from official prompt guidance and behavioural science principles without copying source programme content.
